_meta.notes: An apple orchard where the river finally won. Five rooms half-submerged. The trees still bear; the apples taste of silt and old iron. Designed to flex for forms that float, climb, ooze, or rest on water.

tagline: The river took the orchard last spring. The apples don't know yet.
ambientPool: Ripples cross the water in patterns no fish made.
The apples on the lowest branches are level with your face.
Somewhere upstream, a wooden gate rattles against its hinge.
Birds you cannot name call to each other across the water.

rooms.summary: Where the dry road ends. Water laps at the last cobbles. Beyond, the orchard stands in three feet of slow current.
ambientPool: A scarecrow lies on its side in the shallows. Its straw is gone.
A boot — only one — has wedged itself between two roots.
The water here is clear enough to see the original orchard floor.
rooms.exitNarrative: You leave the orchard. The road is dry. The road has been dry.

rooms.summary: Apple trees in regular rows, knee-deep. Apples float in the spaces between trunks.
ambientPool: An apple bobs against your awareness.
A trunk creaks somewhere left, settling.
Sunlight comes down through the leaves and reaches the water as a moving net.

exits.narrative: Toward a low building visible through the trees.
Deeper, where the water is darker.

rooms.summary: A one-room cottage. Roof intact, door warped open. Inside, water to the second-from-bottom shelf. Things float that should not float.
ambientPool: A child's drawing is pinned above the high-water line.
The kettle on the stove holds river water; you can taste the iron in it from outside.
Something passed through here recently. Something taller than the door allows.

hazards.narrative: Whatever came through stooped to clear the doorway. The lintel has fresh scrapes.

rooms.summary: The water is over your head here for anything tall. The oldest trees are here. They bear apples that are wrong colors.
ambientPool: An apple as black as wet stone is just within reach.
The trunks down here are wider than you can wrap awareness around.

rooms.summary: A stone river-lock. The gate is open. Water pours through it from the south side; the orchard side is calmer. Someone, sometime, opened this on purpose.
ambientPool: The gate-mechanism is fresh-broken; the iron is bright where the bolt was sheared.
On the south side of the lock, the river runs fast and lower than it should be.
A coin sits on the lip of the gate, head up, not yet washed off.

hazards.narrative: Closing this would slowly drain the orchard. Opening it further would empty it.
